headline: THURSDAY OPEN — Chip cohort rebounds pre-market on China H200 approval story; NVDA +0.6% to $205 leads snap-back from Tuesday's -1T rout
summary: Thursday July 9, 2026 pre-open — the AI/semi cohort is rebounding after Tuesday's session that wiped >$1T in market cap. Nvidia (NVDA) trades around $205.30 at 7:38 a.m. ET, +0.6% from Wednesday's $204.12 close (Wed closed +3.65% on the initial H200 leak). Broader semi tape green: AMD, INTC, MU, WDC, SNDK all higher pre-market. The dominant catalyst is Wednesday-evening reporting from The Information (confirmed by Bloomberg) that Beijing will let Alibaba, ByteDance and DeepSeek buy a limited number of Nvidia H200 processors, with the initial ceiling reportedly under 200,000 units (less than half what the Chinese firms had requested) and use restricted to training only. This is the first meaningful Chinese approval since Trump's Dec 8 2025 licensing framework and 25% U.S. revenue-share went live in mid-January. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for NVDA — clears near-term China overhang and sets an approval precedent that Wall Street bull cases have already partly priced (BofA's 'enhanced Buy at 7-yr-low 18x fwd P/E' pitch from Wed); (2) POSITIVE for AMD (MI325X sits on the same conditional-license list); (3) POSITIVE for TSM as the H200/Blackwell fab customer, though Fri June revenue print is the harder tell; (4) NEUTRAL for AVGO (custom-silicon exposure not gated by H-class China approvals); (5) NEUTRAL-to-POSITIVE for the memory complex (MU, 005930.KS, 000660.KS) — H200 uses HBM3e, so approved training builds pull HBM demand through, though Samsung 'peak-memory' framing remains the counter. Downside binaries still intact: Section 232 report is 8 days past its July 1 statutory deadline, and Beijing's approval mechanic is discretionary and could tighten.

headline: NVDA — China greenlights limited H200 sales to Alibaba, ByteDance, DeepSeek; ceiling ~200k chips, training-only, per Information/Bloomberg
summary: Bloomberg confirmed Wednesday July 8 (citing The Information) that China's cyber-space regulators have informed Alibaba (BABA), ByteDance and DeepSeek they will be permitted to purchase a limited quantity of Nvidia H200 training accelerators. Approved companies must submit chip-count and use-case justifications; total approvals are being capped at under 200,000 units, less than half the aggregate requested earlier in 2026. Approved allocations are restricted to AI-model training — inference workloads must run on domestically produced silicon (Huawei Ascend). The mechanic layers onto the existing U.S. framework: Trump's Dec 8, 2025 announcement conditionally cleared H200/MI325X-class exports; BIS issued the implementing rule Jan 13, 2026 (effective Jan 16), with a 25% U.S.-side revenue-share fee and third-party performance testing required. Nvidia now sits on both approvals — U.S. issuer license + Chinese purchaser license — for the first time. The Information's April 2026 reporting had pegged Chinese demand at 400-500k units through 2027, so the 200k cap approximates 40-50% of that TAM. Reaction: NVDA +3.65% Wednesday close to $204.12; pre-market Thursday +0.6% to $205.30. BofA reiterated Buy and framed the setup as 'enhanced Buy opp'ty at 7-yr low 18x fwd PE.' Read-through: this is the single largest positive datapoint on the China overhang since the January framework took effect; every incremental Chinese-approved SKU is directly incremental to NVDA and AMD's data-center TAM. Downside: Beijing kept discretion to tighten or freeze approvals; the U.S. 25% revenue-share still applies to shipped units.

headline: AVGO — Apple announces $30B+ chip supply agreement with Broadcom through 2031; $1.5B Fort Collins CO facility investment, 15B+ chips
summary: Apple confirmed Wednesday July 8 a multi-year chip supply agreement with Broadcom (AVGO) valued at more than $30 billion, extending the two firms' partnership through 2031 and covering custom silicon plus wireless-connectivity components (FBAR RF filters) across multiple future Apple product generations. Apple will invest $1.5 billion to expand and modernize Broadcom's Fort Collins, Colorado manufacturing facility, and the arrangement covers more than 15 billion chips manufactured in the U.S. AVGO closed Wednesday up ~4%. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for AVGO — locks in Apple, which is roughly 20% of Broadcom's sales, for six additional years and removes the single largest customer-concentration overhang the sell-side has flagged since the AAPL-INTC-modem noise last year (Erste Group's Wed downgrade to Hold was pre-announcement and now looks stranded); (2) POSITIVE for the U.S. onshoring narrative that supports Section 232 offsets — AVGO joins TSM Arizona, INTC IFS, GFS Malta as recipients of hyperscaler/OEM committed volumes; (3) NEUTRAL for AAPL — cost/vertical-integration framing rather than incremental hardware TAM; (4) INDIRECT POSITIVE for ETN/VRT/GEV — expanded Fort Collins fab needs incremental power/cooling capex from the same vendor pool serving DC buildouts.

headline: TSM — June 2026 monthly revenue release Fri July 10 pre-open Taipei; May +30% YoY sets bar for third-consecutive record
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor's monthly revenue report for June 2026 lands Friday July 10 (pre-open Taipei / Thursday-evening U.S.), the first read on supply-side sustain after May's record NT$417B (+30% YoY). May's release was accompanied by Citi lifting its price target to NT$3,800 and predicting >30% revenue growth for 2026, and Bernstein's July 6 $2,300 ASML PT raise (+30% from prior) explicitly cross-references TSM N2/N3 capacity commitments. The June number is the third-straight monthly print that must hold >25% YoY to keep the sell-side up-cycle framing intact; a print in the NT$390-420B band would preserve the trajectory, below NT$380B would break it. Confirming catalysts within the next 7 sessions: (a) Fri July 10 monthly revenue; (b) Thursday July 17 Q2 earnings + full-year guidance revision. Read-through: an in-line-or-better June revenue print is the strongest confirming datapoint the semi bulls will get before Q2 earnings season starts July 17; it directly informs NVDA/AMD/AVGO shipment-run-rate visibility into Q3.

headline: 000660.KS / SKHY — SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR debut Fri July 10 at ~$165.26; up to $29B raise, largest foreign-issuer ADR listing on record
summary: SK Hynix's Nasdaq ADR (ticker SKHY) prices Thursday July 9 and debuts Friday July 10 at a reference price near $165.26 per ADR (10 ADRs = 1 Korean common share; indicative KRW 242,500 ≈ $158-165 range depending on the filing reference date). Deal size: up to $28-29B on 17.79M new common-share issuance, positioning it as the largest ADR listing by a foreign issuer in history. Proceeds are explicitly earmarked for 2027-2029 HBM4/HBM4e capex plus advanced-node DRAM buildout in Cheongju M15X and the Yongin cluster. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for the HBM/memory complex — a successful debut anchors a re-rate template that MU and Samsung both benefit from since peer multiples reset with SKHY's opening print; (2) POSITIVE for ASML, LRCX, KLAC, AMAT — the raise funds a specific capex path that Bernstein's ASML thesis (€80B 2030 revenue) already assumes; (3) NEUTRAL-to-NEGATIVE for MU on relative multiple compression if SKHY opens materially above reference and Wall Street rewrites HBM comps around the Korean issuer; (4) NEGATIVE risk if the debut is soft (below reference), which would validate the 'peak memory' template Samsung Q2's post-beat drawdown set on July 7.

headline: PLTR — D.A. Davidson upgrades Palantir to Buy, price target $175 (from $165), citing Nvidia sovereign-AI alliance and $7.65B FY guide
summary: D.A. Davidson upgraded Palantir (PLTR) to Buy Thursday morning, raising its 12-month price target to $175 from $165 and citing (a) Q1 2026 revenue +85% YoY and management's raised FY guide to ~$7.65B; (b) the strategic alliance with Nvidia that lets government agencies train sovereign AI on their own data using PLTR Foundry / AIP as the data-plane layer; (c) the Palantir-Snowflake data-integration partnership announced earlier this year that connects Snowflake AI Data Cloud to Foundry. The upgrade lands as the enterprise-software cohort has softened alongside chips in this week's rout — PLTR entered Thursday down high-single-digits from its recent highs. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for PLTR House Rating framework — a rare bull-side reset among the software cohort after weeks of valuation debate; (2) INDIRECT POSITIVE for SNOW (Foundry-Snowflake integration amplifies AIP TAM); (3) INDIRECT POSITIVE for NVDA — the sovereign-AI alliance is the sell-side framing that keeps Blackwell tied to a durable non-hyperscaler enterprise-license stream; (4) NEUTRAL for CRM/NOW — Foundry-AIP framing does not yet threaten their agentic-AI stacks in the DA Davidson note.

headline: CCJ — Cameco closes acquisition of TEPCO's 5% Cigar Lake stake; ownership rises to 57.418% at ~C$115.75M cost
summary: Cameco (CCJ) confirmed Thursday July 2 (post-market Wed July 8 formal press release re-issued as 6-K) that it and Orano Canada have closed the acquisition of TEPCO Resources Inc.'s 5% participating interest in the Cigar Lake Joint Venture in northern Saskatchewan. Cameco's stake rises 2.871 points to 57.418%; Orano's rises 2.129 points to 42.582%. Cameco's purchase cost is approximately C$115.75M, subject to closing adjustments. Cigar Lake is the highest-grade producing uranium mine in the world and remains Cameco's lowest-cash-cost pound. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for CCJ — incremental higher-margin production without exploration risk; deal was struck at guided pricing so the near-term realized-price framework (2026 guide $85-89/lb, deliveries 29-32M lbs) is unchanged; (2) INDIRECT POSITIVE for LEU (Centrus) — CCJ's tightening upstream ownership underscores structural constraint on Western fuel-cycle supply, which is the same thesis Centrus HALEU underpins; (3) NEUTRAL for OKLO/SMR since fuel offtake is via long-dated Centrus contracts, not CCJ spot.

headline: SECTION 232 SEMI-DATACENTER REPORT — Commerce deadline now 8 days past; binary overhang keeps semi-cohort in tension
summary: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's statutory July 1, 2026 deadline to deliver a semi-DC market update to President Trump under the January 14 Section 232 proclamation is now 8 calendar days past — with no public status update since July 2. The proclamation's original design gave the President a July window to modify the current 25% tariff on advanced logic chips based on the Commerce update; Lutnick's prior public framing threatens tariffs of up to 100% on Korean and Taiwanese fabs that do not invest incremental U.S. capacity, offset by a 'tariff offset program' rewarding U.S.-onshore builds. The delay reads as either (a) inter-agency negotiation over the onshore-investment offset framework, or (b) political calibration around this week's H200 China approval news that softens the overall chip-policy tone. Read-through to House coverage: NEGATIVE binary risk overhangs the semi cohort — a punitive TSM/Samsung/SK Hynix tariff would compress gross margin across the fabless customer base (NVDA/AMD/AVGO/QCOM) unless offset by fresh U.S.-side onshoring commitments. POSITIVE tail-risk overhangs INTC (Foundry offsets), TSM (Arizona/Kaohsiung commitments already banked), and GFS (Malta/Vermont). Absent an announcement by Friday close, cohort tension will re-price into early next week as the calendar drift itself becomes the story.

headline: MU — Fiscal Q4 2026 print approaches; company-guided $50B ±$1B revenue vs Street $43.58B sets 15% upside gap, Samsung template the tell
summary: Micron (MU) enters the run into its fiscal Q4 2026 print with company guidance for $50B ±$1B revenue, ~86% gross margin, $1.65B opex, and $31 ±$1 non-GAAP EPS on a ~1.15B share count — versus Street revenue consensus of $43.58B (guide implies ~15% upside gap). Fiscal Q4 capex guide is ~$10B (fiscal 2026 total ~$27B), with a flagged $1B FY27 opex step-up already telegraphed. Cloud memory Q3 was $13.77B (+300% YoY); DC memory $11.5B vs $1.53B PY. The Q3 print set the up-cycle template; Q4's binary is whether the stock trades the 'record beat + sell' pattern Samsung established on Q2 prelim Jul 7 (KRW 89.4T OP beat, stock -6-7% Seoul) — that pattern is the single largest sentiment-risk factor Wall Street has flagged for MU. Michael Burry's $1,051 short opened Jul 2 (closed same day $975) remains the bear anchor. Read-through: MU print is the single largest cross-check on the HBM demand curve before SKHY's Friday ADR debut sets new public-market memory comps.
source_name: Micron IR (fiscal Q3 release, forward guidance)
